headlines.description: — James Harden made 10 3-pointers and scored a franchise-record 55 points, and the Los Angeles Clippers beat the Charlotte Hornets 131-116 on Saturday hours after 12-time All-Star point guard Chris Paul
headlines.shortLinkText: Harden erupts for 55, Clippers rout Hornets 131-116 in Paul's likely last game in North Carolina

events.name: New York Knicks at Orlando Magic

headlines.description: — Franz Wagner had 37 points, Desmond Bane scored 27, and the Orlando Magic beat the New York Knicks 133-121 on Saturday night for their sixth win in their past seven games.
headlines.shortLinkText: Franz Wagner leads hot-shooting Magic over Knicks, 133-121

headlines.description: — Kristaps Porzingis scored 29 points and Vit Krejci added 21 off the bench on seven 3-pointers to propel the Atlanta Hawks to a 115-98 victory over the skidding New Orleans Pelicans on Saturday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Porzingis scores 29 as Hawks win 115-98 to hand Pelicans their 9th straight loss

headlines.description: — Nikola Vucevic had 28 points and 12 rebounds, and the Chicago Bulls handed Washington its 14th consecutive loss with a 121-120 victory over the Wizards on Saturday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Vucevic scores 28 points as the Bulls hand the Wizards their 14th straight loss

headlines.description: — Cade Cunningham had 29 points, 10 assists and eight rebounds as the Detroit Pistons capitalized on sizzling shooting to beat the Milwaukee Bucks 129-116 on Saturday night for their 12th straight victory.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cunningham helps Pistons breeze past Bucks 129-116 for 12th consecutive victory

headlines.description: — Russell Westbrook scored 15 of his 21 points in the fourth quarter against his former team, and the Sacramento Kings withstood 44 points from Nikola Jokic to beat the Denver Nuggets 128-123 on Saturday night, ending an eight-game slide.
headlines.shortLinkText: Kings overcome Jokic's 44 points, beat Nuggets 128-123 to end 8-game slide
